plan.description: Optimized pipeline for image editing using ControlNet and prompt conditioning, guided by pose estimation.
plan.architecture: Modular Python script pipeline utilizing deep learning models (ControlNet, Diffusion) for image manipulation.

files.purpose: Centralized configuration management for model paths, input/output directories, and hyperparameters.
Utility functions to safely handle and validate file paths, preventing 'NoneType' errors during path joining.
Handles pose estimation (e.g., OpenPose) and generates the conditioning map (e.g., OpenPose skeleton or depth map).
The core logic: loads ControlNet, runs the diffusion process, and applies prompt conditioning.
The entrypoint script that orchestrates the entire pipeline: loading inputs, calling submodules, and managing the workflow.

existing_files_to_modify.purpose: Modify the main execution flow to use the new path validation utilities and ensure all required paths are checked for None before proceeding.

subtasks.name: Refactor Path Handling and Validation
subtasks.description: Create a dedicated utility module to safely handle file paths, specifically addressing the 'NoneType' error encountered during path joining.

subtasks.name: Update Submodules to Use Safe Paths
subtasks.description: Modify `pose_extractor.py` and `image_editor.py` to replace direct path operations with the validated functions from `path_utils.py`. This ensures that if any input path is None, the module fails gracefully with a clear error instead of a runtime crash.

subtasks.name: Update Main Orchestration Logic
subtasks.description: Update `main.py` to incorporate robust try/except blocks and path validation checks at the start of the pipeline execution, ensuring that all necessary inputs (e.g., input image path, pose model path) are valid before calling the core pipeline functions.

subtasks.name: Testing and Verification
subtasks.description: Run the pipeline with known bad inputs (e.g., missing model files, None paths) to confirm that the new path validation logic catches the error gracefully and provides informative feedback, rather than crashing with the 'NoneType' error.
